branchingScenario.title: PBL: General features of the cardiovascular system — Clinical Reasoning

startScreen.startScreenSubtitle: A newborn baby is noted to have bluish discolouration (cyanosis) within hours of birth. The paediatrician suspects a congenital heart defect involving the fetal circulation.

endScreens.endScreenSubtitle: You correctly identified TGA, understood the ductus arteriosus, vessel wall layers, Windkessel function, and end arteries.
Review the fetal circulation, vessel wall layers, Windkessel function, and end arteries.
Review cardiovascular anatomy: vessel wall layers, elastic vs muscular arteries, arterioles, end arteries, and fetal circulation.

params.text:
Patient Presentation

Newborn male, 39 weeks, central cyanosis (SpO2 65%), tachypnoea, single loud S2, no murmur, 'egg-on-a-string' CXR. Three vessel wall layers: tunica intima, media, adventitia. Ductus arteriosus shunts blood from pulmonary trunk to aorta in fetal life, bypassing non-functional lungs.

feedback.title: Correct!
feedback.subtitle: Aorta from RV, PA from LV — two parallel circuits. Survival depends on ductus arteriosus for mixing.

feedback.title: Unlikely
feedback.subtitle: VSD causes a murmur and is initially acyanotic (left-to-right shunt).

feedback.title: Incorrect
feedback.subtitle: Pneumonia shows infiltrates on CXR and responds to O2. This is cyanotic heart disease.

feedback.title: Not the primary problem
feedback.subtitle: In TGA, the PDA is beneficial — it allows blood mixing between the parallel circuits.

params.text:
Echo Confirms d-TGA

Aorta from RV anteriorly, PA from LV posteriorly. Small PDA providing life-saving mixing. Elastic arteries (aorta) have Windkessel function: stretch in systole, recoil in diastole. Arterioles = resistance vessels. End arteries have no anastomoses; blockage causes infarction.

alternatives.text: IV prostaglandin E1 to keep ductus open + balloon atrial septostomy

feedback.title: Correct!
feedback.subtitle: PGE1 keeps ductus patent for mixing. Septostomy improves atrial mixing. Arterial switch operation within 2 weeks.

feedback.title: Fatal!
feedback.subtitle: The ductus is the lifeline in TGA. Closing it eliminates blood mixing.

feedback.title: Insufficient
feedback.subtitle: The problem is structural, not pulmonary. Oxygen doesn't fix inadequate blood mixing.

params.text:
Key Cardiovascular Anatomy

  • Three vessel wall layers: intima, media, adventitia
  • Windkessel function: elastic arteries stretch/recoil for continuous flow
  • Arterioles: resistance vessels (main determinant of PVR)
  • End artery: no anastomoses, blockage causes infarction
  • Ductus arteriosus: pulmonary trunk to aorta shunt in fetal life
